verdict.answer_first: A starts with a concrete plan for the most common case, 'I'll give you the plan for the most common case', while B starts by inventing a problem, 'this repo (`fable-profile`) isn't your web app'.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a specific technology stack, 'push them to open tabs over Server-Sent Events (SSE)', and justifies it, while B's primary recommendation hinges on a guess, 'Since you already run Supabase elsewhere'.
verdict.fabrication: B invents a repo name, 'this repo (`fable-profile`) isn't your web app', and a technology choice, 'Since you already run Supabase elsewhere'. A makes a reasonable assumption and clearly labels it as such.
verdict.actionability: A provides a complete, phased plan I can start on immediately. B's plan is contingent on its (likely false) Supabase assumption and explicitly asks for more information before it can proceed.
